exercises.name: SuperBand Single Arm Row
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at chest height, and standing facing it.

2. Grasp the band in one hand, with your arm fully extended in front of you.

3. Pull the band towards your armpit, while keeping an "open chest" posture. 

4. Return to the start position under control. Repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Superband Single Arm Shoulder Adduction
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing the band to an anchor point at head height or above. 

2. Stand with the band at your side, holding it with a single arm. 

3. With your arm parallel to the ground, pull the band until it is down by the side of your thigh.

4. Return to the start position, and repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: SuperBand Single Leg Glute Bridge
exercises.description:
1) Lay on the floor with your feet flat and knees bent. Loop the band around your waist, like a seat belt, and secure it beneath a single foot. 

2) Execute the movement by driving through the heel of a single leg, extending your hips upward and raising your glutes off of the ground.

3) Extend as high as possible, pausing for a moment at the top before returning to the start position to repeat.

exercises.name: Superband Split Stance Diagonal Chop
exercises.alternateName: Superband Split Squat hold Diagonal Chop
exercises.description:
Anchor a superband at ground level to a stable object. Stand in a split stance with the leg opposite the anchor point forward. 

Hold the band with both hands, keeping your arms extended. Engage your core and pull the band upward and diagonally across your body, finishing above and slightly outside your opposite shoulder. 

Control the movement as you bring the band back down toward the starting position near your hip.

exercises.name: Superband Squat
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright placing both feet inside the resistance band, about shoulder-width apart. Loop the band around your upper back and shoulders.

2.Sit back with your hips, descending into a squat. 

3.Stand upright returning to starting position. Repeat.

exercises.name: Superband Standing Clam Shell
exercises.description:
Anchor a superband at ground level and loop the other end around the thigh (above the knee) of the leg opposite the anchor. 

Stand with feet roughly shoulder-width apart, the banded leg positioned slightly behind the other leg, knees slightly bent and core engaged. 

Open the banded leg outward and upward, maintaining tension in the band, then slowly return to the starting position.

exercises.name: Superband Standing Kick Back
exercises.description:
Anchor a superband at ground level and loop the other end around one foot. Stand facing the anchor in an athletic stance, with your weight slightly on the opposite leg and core engaged. 

Keeping your upper body stable, drive the banded leg straight back and slightly upward, fully extending your hip. 

Pause briefly at the top, then return to the starting position under control.

exercises.name: SuperBand Standing Row
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at about head height, and standing facing it.

2. Grasp the band in boths hand, with your arms fully extended in front of you.

3. Pull the band towards your armpits, while keeping an "open chest" posture. 

4. Return to the start position under control. Repeat.

exercises.name: Superband Standing Step Over Kick Back
exercises.alternateName: banded cross over glute kick back
exercises.description:
Anchor a superband at ground level and loop the other end around one foot. Stand facing the anchor with core engaged. 

Use the non-banded leg to step laterally over the band, then immediately drive the banded leg straight back and slightly upward, fully extending the hip.

exercises.name: SuperBand Stationary Sprint
exercises.description:
1. Start by securing a band to an anchor point at foot height. 

2. Place the band around your waist while standing facing away from the anchor. 

3. Attempt to sprint forward by driving your knees into the air, and quickly alternating, without letting the band pull you backwards.

exercises.name: SuperBand Thruster
exercises.description:
1. Start by standing with feet shoulder-width apart, on the band, and the band in both hands. 

2. Squat down by reaching your hips back and bending your knees. Push yourself back upwards, using momentum to press the band directly up overhead. 

3. Slowly return to the start position under control and repeat.

exercises.name: SuperBand Thrusters
exercises.description:
1. Start by standing with feet shoulder-width apart, on the band, and the band in both hands. 

2. Squat down by reaching your hips back and bending your knees. Push yourself back upwards, using momentum to press the band directly up overhead. 

3. Slowly return to the start position under control and repeat.

exercises.name: SuperBand Upright Row
exercises.description:
1) Stand upright with your feet shoulder-width apart and the centre of the band.

2) Grasp the handles so that your palms are facing inward and touching your upper legs with your arms fully extended, elbows slightly bent.

3) Keeping the handles close to your body, exhale as you raise them straight up until they are just under your chin.

4) Hold for a moment and then inhale as you reverse the motion back to the starting position. Repeat.

exercises.name: Superman
exercises.description:
1.Lie face down, legs straight, feet together, arms out straight above your head on a mat.

2.Squeeze your glutes to slowly raise your legs and arms, as well as slowly arching your back.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Superman Around the World
exercises.description:
Lie face down on the floor with arms extended overhead and legs straight. Engage your core and lift your arms, chest, and legs off the ground into a “superman” position. 

From here, rotate your arms in a controlled circular motion, moving from the extended overhead position down toward your lower back until your hands nearly meet, then bring them back overhead following the same circular path. 

Keep your torso and legs elevated and stable throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Superman Lat Pull
exercises.description: Lie face down with arms extended overhead. Lift your chest and arms off the ground, then pull your elbows toward your sides in a lat pull motion. Return to start.

exercises.name: Superman Push Up
exercises.description:
1.Lie face down, legs straight, feet together, palms on the floor and elbows bent.

2.Push upwards as powerfully as possible from both your hands and feet, propelling yourself upwards into the air, they have to be off the ground. Extend your arms forward while in the air so that your body is in a straight line.

3.Land back into starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Supinated cable bar Tricep pull down
Supine Active Straight Leg Raise
exercises.description: Lie on your back and actively lift one leg straight up while keeping the other on the ground.

exercises.name: Supine Ankle Circles
exercises.description: Lie on your back and rotate your ankle in circles to increase mobility.

exercises.name: Supine Chin Tucks
exercises.description:
1. Lie face up on a mat with your hands by your sides. 

2. Draw your chin downwards towards the mat to create a neutral neck position.

3. While holding a neutral neck position, lightly lift your head 1 inch up off the mat. 

4. Hold for desired time or repeat for desired number of repetitions.

exercises.name: Supine Hip Axial Rotations
exercises.alternateName: single leg hip internal external mobility
exercises.description: Lie on your back and rotate your hips inward and outward while keeping your legs extended.

exercises.name: Supine Hip Flexion Hold
exercises.description: Same as the active version but using your hands to assist in holding the position.

exercises.name: Supine Hip Flexion Hold (Active)
exercises.alternateName: active stretch single knee to chest
exercises.description: Lie on your back and hold one knee toward your chest with active engagement.

exercises.name: Supported Single Leg Squat
exercises.description:
1.Stand on one foot with your hands on a seat or bench in front at chest height.

2.Lower your body towards the floor, pushing your hips back and down and bending your knee, using the seat/bench for support.

3.Push through your heel returning to starting position. Keep your back flat and your head up throughout the movement.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Suspended pistol squat
exercises.description: You can use a cable machine if your gym doesn’t have a TRX

exercises.name: Suspension Alternating Lunge Jump
exercises.alternateName: TRX Alternating Reverse Lunge With Hop
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, ensure that you have a tall upper body and that when you land, you land on soft legs rather than locked out knees.
 
2.As best you can, ensure that your body weight is over top of your back leg and the lower body limb segments are about 90 degrees.

3.Take a step to the rear, allowing your hips and knees to flex to lower your body. 

4.Contacting the back leg through only the ball of the foot, descend until your knee nearly touches the ground. This will be your starting position.

5.Hop by pushing yourself up, then switching the legs returning to starting position.

exercises.name: Suspension Alternating Oblique Crunch
exercises.alternateName: TRX Oblique side to side Crunch
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, you’ll wanna maintain neutral spine in the lengthen position.

2.When conducting the oblique twist, what you’ll wanna do is bring the bottom of the ribs from one side towards the hips on the other.

3.With your feet suspended on the straps, begin the exercise with arms extended, back and neck in a straight line, and core tight. This will be your starting position. 

4.Pull your legs to the side with your knees bent. During this movement, your hips will rise upward. Then back to starting position.

5.Repeat for the recommended number of repetitions.

exercises.name: Suspension Alternating Reverse Lunge
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, aim to have your lower body segments hit 90 degrees. 

2.Ensure to load up the back leg by keeping a tall body as well.

3.Take a step to the rear, allowing your hips and knees to flex to lower your body. 

4.Contacting the back leg through only the ball of the foot, descend until your knee nearly touches the ground. This will be your starting position. 

5.Pushing yourself up, then switching the legs returning to starting position.

exercises.name: Suspension Alternating Side Lunge
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, you wanna ensure that your hip, knee and foot are all in line on the side that you step up with. 

2.The other leg will remain straight. 

3.Also, ensure that your hip sit back. And that as you stand, you engage your glutes as you stand up.

exercises.name: Suspension Assisted Slider Lunge
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, keep your core engaged. You’ll also want all your lower body segments to hit about 90 degrees. 

2. Grab the straps, position hands near the chest and stand on the sliders. This will be the starting position.

3.While holding onto the straps as support, slide one leg backwards until your knee nearly touches the ground while making sure the other leg hit 90 degrees. 

4.Pull yourself up then back to starting position.

4.Repeat for the recommended amount of repetitions. 

5.When you complete one side, do the other.

exercises.name: Suspension Atomic Push Up
exercises.description:
1.When you perform this exercise, you’ll wanna ensure that you keep your hands and elbows in line mid chest.

2.You’ll also wanna ensure in the push up position that you keep your abdominals on and set so that your lower back doesn’t arch.

3.With your feet suspended on the straps, begin in the "up" position of a push-up: arms extended, back and neck in a straight line, and core tight. This will be your starting position.

4.Descend into the bottom position of the push-up by flexing at the elbows until they reach a 90-degree position. Then back to starting position.

5. Then, pull your legs towards your chest with your knees slightly bent. During this movement, your hips will rise upward. Continue the movement until your hips are over your shoulders in an inverted press position. Back to starting position.

6.Repeat for the recommended number of repetitions.

exercises.name: Suspension Bent Leg Raise
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, ensure your abdominals are active. 

2.This will be particularly challenging when your feet are closer to the ground.

3.To help keep your abdominals engaged, press down on to the handles to ensure an active body while performing this exercise.

4.Lie with your back flat on the ground, hold the handles with your arms straight and hands above your torso. Bend your knees. This will be your starting position.

5.With your knees bent and locked, raise your legs until they make a 90-degree angle with the floor. Exhale as you perform this portion of the movement and hold the contraction at the top for a second.

6.As you inhale, slowly lower your legs back down to the starting position.

exercises.name: Suspension Bicep Clutch
exercises.description:
1.For this exercise, you wanna keep your core engaged and glutes on.

2.You’re gonna be working your biceps as you pull your hands towards your chest keeping your elbows up.

3. Facing the suspension system, grasp the handles with a supinated grip. With elbows up and your hands in front of your chest holding the handles, this will be your starting position.

4. Perform the movement by leaning back allowing your elbows to straighten then pulling yourself upward back to the starting position.

5.To make this exercise easier, you walk your feet away from the straps. To make it more challenging, walk them towards them.

exercises.name: Suspension Bicep Curl
exercises.description:
1.For this exercise, keep your core engaged and glutes on to maintain neutral spine.

2.You’ll be working your biceps so keep your elbows high as you pull your hand towards your body.

3.Facing the suspension system, grasp the handles with a supinated (underhand) grip. 

4.Keeping your body rigid, lean back allowing your elbows to straighten. This will be your starting position.

5.Perform the movement by flexing the elbows, pulling yourself upward. Then, lower yourself to the staring position.

6.To make this exercise more challenging, more your feet closer to the strap. If you wanna make it easier, move your feet away.

exercises.name: Suspension Bicycle Crunch
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, you wanna ensure that you cross the opposing shoulder to the opposite side of your body as best as you can.

2.The easiest way to think about that is moving the opposite shoulder to the opposite knee.

3.Lie flat on the floor with your lower back pressed to the ground with your feet suspended on the straps. 

4.Lift your shoulders into the crunch position. Bring knees up to where they are perpendicular to the floor, with your lower legs parallel to the floor. This will be your starting position.

5.Simultaneously, slowly go through a cycle pedal motion kicking forward with the right leg and bringing in the knee of the left leg. 

6.Bring your right elbow close to your left knee by crunching to the side, as you breathe out.

7.Go back to the initial position as you breathe in.

8.Crunch to the opposite side as you cycle your legs and bring closer your left elbow to your right knee and exhale.

9.Continue alternating in this manner until all of the recommended repetitions for each side have been completed.
